chapter_context.summary: The final allotments for Simeon, Zebulun, Issachar, Asher, Naphtali, and Dan.

outline.explanation: This section focuses on Inheritance for Simeon (19:1-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Joshua.
This section focuses on Zebulun (19:10-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Joshua.
This section focuses on Issachar (19:17-23), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Joshua.
This section focuses on Asher (19:24-31), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Joshua.
This section focuses on Naphtali (19:32-39), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Joshua.
This section focuses on Dan (19:40-48), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Joshua.
This section focuses on Joshua's Inheritance (19:49-51), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Joshua.

key_themes.title: Courage
key_themes.description: The requirement to be 'strong and courageous' in the face of giants.

key_themes.title: Faithfulness
key_themes.description: God's fulfillment of His promises to give Israel the land.

devotional.question: What 'Jericho walls' in your life are you trusting God to bring down?
How can you build 'memorial stones' to remember God's victories in your journey?

cultural_historical.title: The Conquest
cultural_historical.text: The book of Joshua records the transition from a wandering nation to a settled one, fulfilling the patriarchal promises.
